summary: Comprehensive unit test coverage of all major functions (7 test groups, 27 tests) with proper integration testing. Tests cover version parsing (text and JSON), commit classification (all bump types and precedence), changelog generation, file updates, and workflow structure validation. Well-organized with clear naming and proper Pester patterns (BeforeAll, AfterAll, AfterEach cleanup). Strengths: all key requirements tested, bump precedence logic thoroughly verified, good fixture management. Weaknesses: limited edge case coverage (malformed JSON, special characters, whitespace handling), no parameterized tests for similar cases, missing error scenarios for Set-SemanticVersion and New-ChangelogEntry functions, no boundary condition tests (0.0.0, very large versions). Would catch most regressions but some edge cases could escape.
